Figure 14.title: Total number of Companies Registered on the Isle of Man

rows.12 Months to: The data is calculated on the basis of a 12 month rolling average to the end of each quarter.

rows.Vessels: Figures from 2019 are from 31 March. Figuires prior to 2019 are 31 December.

rows.Year: A methodological change has been applied to the average earnings from 2012 and therefore may not be directly comparable to 2011 and earlier.
The Earnings Survey relates to pay for June.
Earnings figures only include full time employees, on adult rates, whose earnings were not affected by absence.
A full time employee is classified as anyone working over 30 hours a week, with the exception of teachers.
The median is the level of earnings such that half the sample have earnings greater than that level.
A methodological change has been applied to the average earnings from 2012 and therefore may not be directly comparable to 2011 and earlier.
The Earnings Survey relates to pay for June.
Earnings figures only include full time employees, on adult rates, whose earnings were not affected by absence.
A full time employee is classified as anyone working over 30 hours a week, with the exception of teachers.

rows.Earnings Bracket: The Earnings Survey relates to pay for June.
Earnings figures only include full time employees, on adult rates, whose earnings were not affected by absence.
A full time employee is classified as anyone working over 30 hours a week, with the exception of teachers.

rows.Source: Source: Isle of Man Annual Financial Statements and Report
The Isle of Man RPI as at March 2020 has been used to calculate constant prices.

Figure 78.title: Bathing Water Quality at Island Beaches

rows.Year: 2022 data was not available at time of publishing.

rows.Monitoring period: Source: Department of Environment, Food and Agriculture

Because of COVID the bathing water monitoring was reduced significantly in 2020, and in 2021 there was a reduction in number of samples as there are now only 4 designated beaches. Seven samples were collected on a weekly basis from Port Erin, Port st Mary, Douglas Central, Douglas Broadway, Douglas Summerhill, Ramsey South and Ramsey North, Castletown was added in 2021.
